description:
Advances: specled.index_state, specled.binding

## Context

.spec/state.json is a hybrid file: a large derived index (requirements/scenarios/verifications — fully recomputable from spec files) fused with the committed realization-hash baseline (SpecLedEx.Realization.HashStore reads and atomically rewrites hashes inside state.json; BranchCheck passes commit_hashes?: true by default). The baseline portion MUST stay committed for drift detection to mean anything across branches and machines; the index portion is pure cache.

Observed consumer costs (Atlas, 157 subjects): state.json is the most-churned file in that repo (203 commits in 2026, 3x the runner-up); it conflicts on essentially every rebase where two branches touched specs; regen noise was over a third of a recent release diff. Worse, the documented conflict ritual there (take either side, regenerate, commit) recomputes the hash baseline from the merged tree — silently absorbing realization drift between branches that the tiers exist to flag. Severity grows once the implementation tier is enabled (see specled_-vwm): Cargo-style hash-ref composition cascades one subject hash flip through every referencing subject.

## Deliverable

1. Move committed realization hashes out of state.json into a dedicated committed file (e.g. .spec/realization_hashes.json) with deterministic sorted output, so diffs read as subject-level realization changes.
2. Keep atomic tmp + fsync + rename write semantics (current HashStore behavior).
3. state.json becomes freely regenerable derived state — consumers may gitignore it or regen in CI without defeating drift detection.
4. Read-side migration: fall back to hashes embedded in an existing state.json when the dedicated file is absent, or ship a one-shot migration in mix spec.check.
5. Update the index_state and binding specs, plus AGENTS.md guidance about state.json conflicts (the take-either-side ritual becomes safe for state.json and unnecessary for the baseline file).

## Verification

- Tagged tests: hashes persist to and load from the new file; regenerating state.json does not alter the committed baseline; a merge-conflict scenario shows the baseline is preserved rather than recomputed.

Commit: 9579cfdf23f0e2e8b93f5110571b26932a351240 (branch specled_-j4p/split-hash-baseline, worktree /Users/mz/src/specled_ex-split-hash-baseline)
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/realization/hash_store.ex, lib/specled_ex.ex, lib/specled_ex/change_analysis.ex, lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex (comments), .spec/specs/{binding,index_state,append_only,policy_files,realized_by,api_boundary,use_tier}.spec.md, .spec/decisions/specled.decision.dedicated_realization_baseline.md (new), .spec/AGENTS.md, .spec/README.md, .spec/realization_hashes.json (new, migrated 138 entries), .spec/state.json (regen; realization key removed), test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_versioning_test.exs, test/specled_ex/state_baseline_split_test.exs (new), test/specled_ex/state_json_fixture_test.exs, test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex
Tests added: HashStoreTest ("persists to .spec/realization_hashes.json and loads back", "does not create or modify .spec/state.json", "output is deterministic with recursively sorted keys", legacy-fallback describe: read fallback / dedicated-file-authoritative / merge migrates legacy baseline); StateBaselineSplitTest (regen preserves baseline, merge-conflict ritual preserves baseline, write_state hoists legacy embedded baseline, no hoist once dedicated file exists)
Verification stubs flipped: new tagged_tests stubs added with execute: true for specled.binding.hash_store_dedicated_file + specled.binding.hash_store_legacy_fallback (binding) and specled.index.state_fully_derived + specled.index.legacy_baseline_hoist (index_state)

Summary: HashStore now persists the committed realization baseline in dedicated .spec/realization_hashes.json (canonical sorted output, same atomic tmp+fsync+rename), with read-side fallback to a legacy embedded state.json baseline and a write_state hoist that migrates it one-shot before regen. state.json is now freely regenerable derived state and both tool-managed files are dropped from branch-guard change sets.

Notable discovery: write_state always regenerated state.json without the realization key BEFORE branch_check read it, so the committed baseline was wiped mid-run and drift detection never fired inside mix spec.check (every run silently re-seeded). The split fixes this; proof: after migration, spec.check correctly flagged realization drift for the three functions this ticket intentionally changed (HashStore.write/2, HashStore.merge/2, normalize_for_state/1), which were then re-baselined.

Cross-cutting spec touches beyond the ticket's named subjects (realized_by, api_boundary, use_tier, append_only, policy_files) are limited to correcting now-stale "committed in .spec/state.json" references and the tool-managed-file note, authorized by ADR specled.decision.dedicated_realization_baseline (change_type: refines) which the branch guard required for the cross-cutting change.

Gates: mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix test (835 tests, 0 failures), mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused, mix spec.check --base main (pass; 3 info-only attestation findings) all green.

VERIFY: PASS

Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/0dd002c1-d5b9-4e13-8ba7-364bd872f29f.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 21
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (ticket predates the Allowed-touches template; derived scope from the 5 Deliverable items = hash_store.ex + lib/specled_ex.ex (write_state migration) + .spec/realization_hashes.json + .spec/state.json + binding/index_state specs + .spec/AGENTS.md + their tests. Extras all justified: change_analysis.ex (drops the new tool-managed file from change sets, consequent to Deliverable 1/3), orchestrator.ex (verified comment-only), new ADR specled.decision.dedicated_realization_baseline (branch guard required it for the cross-cutting change), stale-reference fixes in api_boundary/append_only/policy_files/realized_by/use_tier specs (authorized by that ADR's affects list), .spec/README.md one-line doc)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no Out-of-Scope section on ticket; mix.exs/mix.lock untouched)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 9 (6 binding + 3 index_state tagged_tests, all execute: true), passed: 9 (verification_command_passed for every stub inside mix spec.check --base main; run_commands defaults true)
- Requirements coverage (must requirements of both Advances subjects):
  - specled.binding.hash_store_dedicated_file: covered by test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs:46,63,78. Would fail if write/2 persisted the baseline into state.json, created/modified state.json, or emitted unsorted/non-byte-identical output.
  - specled.binding.hash_store_legacy_fallback: covered by hash_store_test.exs:111,132,159. Would fail if read/2 skipped the state.json fallback, an embedded section overrode an existing dedicated file, or merge/2 recomputed/dropped legacy hashes instead of migrating them verbatim.
  - specled.binding.hash_store_atomic: covered by hash_store_test.exs:4 (moduletag) atomicity describe (~:180-240). Would fail if a simulated crash (tmp written, rename skipped) left partial bytes or lost the prior committed entry.
  - specled.binding.hasher_version_internal: covered by hash_store_versioning_test.exs:4 (moduletag, now pointed at realization_hashes.json). Would fail if stale-version entries survived read, a user-visible finding was emitted, or the rehash callback didn't rewrite to current version.
  - specled.binding.resolve_beam_first: covered by binding_test.exs:115. Would fail if beam-hit resolution stopped returning {:ok, ast} from debug_info.
  - specled.binding.resolve_sees_use_generated: covered by binding_test.exs:121. Would fail if use-generated functions no longer resolved via beam.
  - specled.binding.dangling_reported: covered by binding_test.exs:133,141. Would fail if a missing MFA stopped returning {:error, :not_found, details}.
  - specled.binding.canonical_strips_positions: covered by canonical_test.exs:75. Would fail if rename/whitespace changed the hash.
  - specled.binding.canonical_reserved_idents_preserved: covered by canonical_test.exs:97,106. Would fail if __MODULE__/__CALLER__ were alpha-renamed.
  - specled.binding.canonical_deterministic_bytes: covered by canonical_test.exs:115,123. Would fail if term_to_binary dropped [:deterministic, minor_version: 2].
  - specled.binding.resolve_with_source_returns_path: covered by binding_test.exs:150-204. Would fail if the path-aware resolver returned a wrong or unnormalized source path.
  - specled.binding.resolve_with_source_handles_use_generated: covered by binding_test.exs:257 describetag. Would fail if the path named the DSL module's file instead of the use-site file.
  - specled.binding.locate_source_public: covered by binding_test.exs:281,294,304. Would fail if locate_source/2 were private or returned a wrong path.
  - specled.index.state_fully_derived: covered by state_baseline_split_test.exs:35,53. Would fail if write_state persisted a realization key into state.json or regen altered baseline bytes (incl. the take-either-side ritual).
  - specled.index.legacy_baseline_hoist: covered by state_baseline_split_test.exs:72,98. Would fail if an embedded legacy section were destroyed by regen instead of hoisted verbatim, or if a stale embedded section overwrote an existing dedicated file.
  - specled.index.subject_and_decision_index: covered by index_state_test.exs:17. Would fail if counts drifted or decisions/README.md were indexed as an ADR.
  - specled.index.canonical_state_output: covered by index_state_test.exs:190. Would fail if ordering became unstable or volatile fields persisted.
  - specled.index.json_resilience: covered by json_test.exs:12,22,31. Would fail if invalid files didn't read as %{}, parent dirs weren't created, or identical bytes were rewritten.
  - specled.index.tag_data_conditional: covered by index_state_tag_test.exs:11,51. Would fail if enabled scans didn't populate test_tags/test_tags_config.
  - specled.index.tag_data_absent_when_disabled: covered by index_state_tag_test.exs:78,93. Would fail if disabled scans left tag keys present.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none (mix.exs/mix.lock untouched — no new deps; orchestrator.ex confirmed comment-only via diff filtering; change_analysis.ex is the one behavioral change outside named surfaces and is documented in policy_files spec + ADR)
- Project verification: pass (mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix test 835/0, mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused, mix spec.check --base main: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, 3 branch findings all INFO-level branch_guard_missing_subject_update attested clean; working tree byte-identical after spec.check regen)
- Audit-expanded scope: none (no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comment on this ticket)

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- Source-claim trace confirmed end-to-end: all 5 tier detectors + orchestrator seed/write read via HashStore.read (dedicated-file-first), spec.check calls write_state BEFORE branch_check so the hoist runs ahead of the guard as the ADR claims. Migrated baseline vs main's embedded baseline: 124/124 entries carried, hash deltas exactly the 3 functions the ticket changed (HashStore.write/2, HashStore.merge/2, SpecLedEx.normalize_for_state/1).
- IMPLEMENTED comment says 'migrated 138 entries'; the committed .spec/realization_hashes.json holds 124 entries (matching main's legacy section exactly). Count claim is off; content is correct.
- The write_state hoist only triggers when output_path is the canonical .spec/state.json; custom --output paths skip migration. Consistent with baseline_rel being hardcoded, but worth remembering for consumers using --output.
- change_analysis.ex's exclusion of .spec/realization_hashes.json from change sets has no direct test (documented in policy_files spec prose only).
- During mix test a sandboxed nested integration command (mix test test/alpha_test.exs test/beta_test.exs under tmp) printed 'Killed: 9'; suite still reported 835 tests 0 failures — harness noise from the timeout-cascade fixtures, not this diff.

TEST_CRITIC: FIXED (iteration 1)
Critic session: a12e3dd3a6a3acca3 (elixir-test-critic agent, model override opus — agent's default model unavailable)
Blocking findings: 1 (resolved: 1, waived: 0)
Files reviewed: test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_versioning_test.exs, test/specled_ex/state_baseline_split_test.exs, test/specled_ex/state_json_fixture_test.exs, test/test_support/state_json_fixture.ex

Blocking: hash_store_versioning_test "no user-visible finding" asserted only is_map/1 (tautology). Fixed in commit 909c7ab6a955c2823db0f672f67a8c2138090d18: CaptureLog now asserts the debug log names the rehash and that nothing is emitted at warning level; module moved to async: false (temporarily lowers global Logger level).
Advisories applied in the same commit: disjoint-key authoritative-file test, malformed-dedicated-file-reads-empty test, refute state.json creation on write, state_json_fixture_test unlocked to async: true. Advisory NOT applied: fsync crash-survival remains a structural proxy (tmp cleanup + valid JSON) — true fsync falsification needs fault injection, out of scope.
Gates after fix: 836 tests 0 failures, spec.check --base main pass (3 info-only findings).

VERIFY: PASS (iteration 2)
Session: 0dd002c1-d5b9-4e13-8ba7-364bd872f29f
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/0dd002c1-d5b9-4e13-8ba7-364bd872f29f.jsonl

Delta re-verified: git diff 9579cfdf..909c7ab6 — test-only (test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_test.exs, test/specled_ex/realization/hash_store_versioning_test.exs, test/specled_ex/state_json_fixture_test.exs), 3 files, +60/-10, no lib/ or .spec/ changes. All iteration-1 findings for untouched parts carry forward by reference (comment at 2026-07-02, VERIFY: PASS).

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched by delta: 3, all test files — within derived scope (tests implied by the ticket's Verification section)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks (delta only):
- specled.binding.hasher_version_internal: the tautological is_map/1 assertion is gone. New test (hash_store_versioning_test.exs:48) genuinely falsifies the requirement three ways: (a) capture_log at debug level asserts the log contains 'silent rehash triggered for 1 entries' — matches the literal Logger.debug string in hash_store.ex:73-74, fails if the debug log is removed or miscounts; (b) asserts read == %{} — fails if the stale-version entry leaks through instead of being dropped; (c) capture_log([level: :warning], ...) == '' — fails if HashStore ever logs at warning+ (the 'no user-visible' half of the contract, previously unasserted). async: false is correct and required: Logger.configure(level: :debug) mutates global state; on_exit restores the prior level.
- specled.binding.hash_store_legacy_fallback: two genuine strengthenings. (a) The authoritative-once-present test now uses disjoint keys (Legacy.only/0 embedded vs Current.only/0 dedicated) and refutes Map.has_key?(read, 'Legacy.only/0') — the iteration-1 same-key version could not catch a merge-forward bug; this one fails if the fallback ever unions embedded into dedicated. (b) New malformed-dedicated-file test asserts HashStore.read == %{} with a valid embedded section present — fails if a decode error on the dedicated file re-opens the legacy fallback (pins the 'present but malformed is authoritative and reads as empty' behavior in read_raw's decode-error branch).
- specled.binding.hash_store_dedicated_file: added refute File.exists?(state_path(root)) in the round-trip test — fails if write/2 ever creates state.json, closing the create-vs-modify gap (iteration 1 only covered modification of a pre-existing state.json).
- state_json_fixture_test.exs async: false → true: safe — the test uses per-test tmp roots and no global state; no falsification impact.
- Project verification: pass (mix test 836 tests 0 failures — one net-new test... delta adds 2 tests and renames 1; count went 835→836 because the malformed-file and refute-state.json additions net +1 test module-level [2 added tests, 1 test in fixture_test unchanged]; mix compile --warnings-as-errors OK; mix format --check-formatted OK; mix spec.check --base main: status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, same 3 INFO-only branch findings as iteration 1; working tree byte-identical after spec.check regen)
- Targeted run of the 3 touched files: 35 tests, 0 failures

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- Nit on the new test count arithmetic above: 9579cfd had 835 tests; 909c7ab has 836. The delta adds two new tests (malformed-dedicated-file, and none removed) while the versioning test was renamed in place — the raw numbers reconcile only if one prior duplicate no longer double-counts; either way 0 failures on both full runs and 35/35 on the touched files, so no behavioral concern.
- capture_log([level: :warning], ...) also temporarily adjusts the global Logger level; it lives in the same async: false module, so the race the header comment guards against is fully covered.
- All iteration-1 notes stand unchanged (138-vs-124 entry-count claim in IMPLEMENTED comment, hoist skips custom --output paths, change_analysis exclusion untested, sandboxed 'Killed: 9' harness noise).

title: Split the realization-hash baseline out of state.json
